findings.description: Uses the non-streaming Claude call instead of anthropic.messages.stream(), against the CLAUDE.md hard rule ('Always use anthropic.messages.stream()'). Holds the serverless function open for the full generation instead of streaming/closing early.
findings.impact: Small (3 short alternatives per call) but inconsistent with the rest of the codebase's streaming convention and slightly increases function duration per invocation.
findings.fix: Switch to anthropic.messages.stream() and accumulate server-side before responding, or return an SSE stream to the SwapPanel.

findings.description: Same pattern: no dynamic API referenced, so the ICS-generation GET could be cached by Route Handler default caching.
findings.impact: Lower severity than participants/votes since this is a manually-triggered 'Add to calendar' download and trip data is usually stable/locked by the time it's used, but still worth forcing dynamic for correctness if activities are edited after a calendar link has been shared.
findings.fix: Add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` for consistency with the other trip-scoped GET routes.

findings.description: GET Route Handler never reads request.url, searchParams, cookies(), or headers() — the only input is the resolved `params.id`. Next 14 Route Handlers cache GET responses by default (Full Route Cache) unless a dynamic API or explicit `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` opts out. This endpoint is polled on every trip-page mount to show has_voted/opened_link/last_seen for each participant — if cached, new joins and updated voting status stop showing up for anyone hitting a previously-warmed URL.
findings.impact: High: this is the organiser dashboard's core signal (who has/hasn't voted). A cached response defeats the entire nudge/conflict-tracking feature.
findings.fix: Add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to this route file.

findings.description: Selects all participant columns including push_subscription jsonb blob for a list endpoint that only needs name/initial/color/role/has_voted/opened_link.
findings.impact: Minor extra payload, larger with more participants.
findings.fix: Project needed columns; fetch push_subscription only where used (push send route).

findings.description: Identical Route Handler caching gap as participants: no dynamic API used, so the GET response for a given tripId risks being cached and served stale, freezing vote tallies for every participant. Separately (correctness, not cost): the handler ignores the `activityIds` query param the client sends (`/api/trip/${tripId}/votes?activityIds=...`) and always returns all votes for the trip — dead filter, not itself a cost problem but worth cleaning up alongside the cache fix.
findings.impact: High: vote counts are the product's core mechanic; a cached/stale response makes the UI look broken.

findings.description: 49 posts rendered as next/link with default prefetch behavior (prefetch=true). Next will prefetch the RSC payload for every card as it enters the viewport while scrolling a 3-column grid, so a single visit to /blog can trigger dozens of background prefetch requests for posts the user never opens.
findings.impact: Edge Requests scale with grid size (49 links); grows every time a new post is added per the content backlog in CLAUDE.md.
findings.fix: Set prefetch={false} on the post links (keep default/true only on primary nav CTAs), or prefetch={null} to fall back to hover-intent-only prefetching.

findings.description: Same missing dynamic/revalidate config as /trip/[id]. Additionally, the redirect() at line 61 branches on `trip.status !== 'locked'` — if the page's data is cached from before the organiser locked the trip, participants could be bounced back to the voting view (or vice versa) based on stale status.
findings.impact: Same stale-data risk, compounded by a status-dependent redirect that becomes incorrect once cached.

findings.description: .select('*') on both trips and activities tables, same over-fetch pattern as /trip/[id].

Server component queries Supabase via the standard supabase-js client (which runs on global fetch, patched by Next's Data Cache). No `dynamic`/`revalidate` export exists and no dynamic API (cookies/headers/searchParams) is called anywhere in the tree (NameGate reads localStorage client-side only), so Next may apply its default static/force-cache behavior to these fetches and cache the rendered trip page indefinitely per trip id after first visit. For a page whose entire purpose is showing live vote counts/activity edits, this can silently freeze the page at whatever state it was in on first request.
findings.impact: Correctness risk (stale votes/activities shown) plus mis-attributed ISR Reads; fix is nearly free (one line) and prevents a support-ticket-generating bug.
findings.fix: Add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to app/trip/[id]/page.tsx (mirrors the swap/regenerate routes which already force-dynamic).

findings.description: Both queries pull every column, including trips.intake_data (jsonb, only needed by the regenerate endpoint) and activities.ai_rationale/tags/booking_ref which aren't all needed for first paint.
findings.impact: Extra bytes moved origin→function on every trip view; compounds once the caching bug above is fixed and every view becomes a real DB roundtrip.
findings.fix: Project only the columns TripClient/ConfirmedClient actually render, e.g. .select('id,name,destination,dates_start,dates_end,nights,golfers_count,partners_count,status,organiser_uuid').

config_findings.description: No images config block, and repo-wide there is zero next/image usage, zero raw tags, and no public/ assets over ~20KB (favicon.ico, placeholder.svg, two verification .txt files, sw.js). No .mp4/.gif files exist in public/.
config_findings.impact: Currently a non-issue — no image-driven Fast Data Transfer cost at all. Flagged as info so the convention (next/image + sizes + explicit quality, avif/webp formats) is applied from the start once hero/OG imagery is added (CLAUDE.md backlog item: 'Implement next/image for all images across the site').
config_findings.fix: No action needed now; adopt next/image with images.formats: ['image/avif','image/webp'] when real images are introduced.

config_findings.description: No middleware.ts exists in the repo.
config_findings.impact: N/A — nothing to flag; noted explicitly so this isn't mistaken for an unchecked item.

config_findings.description: All destination and blog routes are literal folders (app/destinations/scottsdale/page.tsx etc.), not [slug] dynamic segments, so the 'dynamic route with no generateStaticParams' check does not apply — these pages are already fully static at build time (best-case ISR posture).
config_findings.impact: None — informational, confirms this portion of the audit's ISR checklist is already satisfied by the current architecture.
config_findings.fix: None required. Keep new destination/blog pages as literal static routes rather than introducing a [slug] pattern unless a real CMS pipeline is added.

config_findings.description: No revalidateTag/revalidatePath usage anywhere. CLAUDE.md references a hypothetical scheduled content-generation GitHub Actions pipeline, but .github/workflows/build.yml is CI-only (lint/typecheck/test/build on push) — there is no content pipeline currently writing blog/destination pages at runtime.
config_findings.impact: None currently; on-demand revalidation isn't needed while content ships as committed static route files. Worth revisiting only if a real CMS/content-automation pipeline is added later.

config_findings.description: Both export `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and correctly use anthropic.messages.stream(). Given these are POST-only, personalized, streaming AI-generation endpoints, force-dynamic is appropriate here, not a bug.
config_findings.impact: None — included for completeness per the audit's instruction to note context rather than assume force-dynamic is always wrong.

config_findings.description: TripProvider's init() (lines ~70-101) awaits the participants GET, then conditionally POSTs a join, then awaits the votes GET — all sequentially — even though the votes fetch only depends on `initialActivities` (a prop) and does not depend on the participants response.
config_findings.impact: Adds one full round-trip of latency to every trip page load and is a textbook fetch waterfall; doesn't change total request count but increases time-to-interactive and, combined with the Route Handler caching bugs above, is one more variable making the voting UI feel unreliable.
config_findings.fix: Fire the participants fetch and the votes fetch concurrently (Promise.all), joining conditionally afterward based on the participants result.
